reasoning_chain_text: 1. Identify the New York City borough described as the northernmost borough.

2. For that borough, determine the 2021 NYPD precinct with the most shooting cases, then identify the most common non-null 2021 location-position category in NYPD complaint incidents for that same borough.
3. Return the 2022 shooting-case count in that borough for the location-position category from step 2.
original_final_question: I’m interested in the northernmost borough of New York City. Within that borough, identify the NYPD precinct with the most shooting cases in 2021. Then, for that same borough and precinct, count how many shooting cases in 2022 occurred in the same location-position category as the most common (non-null) location-position category in complaint incidents reported by NYPD in 2021. Only report the 2022 count. Your response should be in the format [Answer].
original_reasoning_chain: Node 1: From Wikipedia, identify the borough described as the northernmost of NYC’s five boroughs -> BRONX.
Node 2 depends on node 1: Using borough BRONX as the filter, find the 2021 shooting-incident precinct with the most incidents -> 44.

Node 3 depends on nodes 1 and 2: Using the same borough (BRONX) and precinct (44), switch to 2021 complaint data to select a concrete, checkable location context via the most common non-null LOC_OF_OCCUR_DESC -> INSIDE.
Node 4 depends on nodes 1 and 3: Carry that LOC_OF_OCCUR_DESC value (INSIDE) into the 2022 shooting-incident data for the same borough and count matching incidents -> 64.
